learning.evaluation_split_origin: R2MED benchmark release sampled into NanoR2MED
learning.leakage_note: exclude R2MED IIYi-Clinical evaluation queries, qrels, and positive translated case records
useful_training_data: translated or native-English clinical case retrieval
hard negatives with similar chief complaints but different diagnoses

synthetic_data.document_generation: structured de-identified clinical cases with diagnosis-bearing outcomes
synthetic_data.question_generation: diagnosis-removed patient records asking for similar supporting cases
synthetic_data.answerability: positives should share the diagnosis or provide diagnostically useful support
synthetic_data.hard_negatives: similar demographics or chief complaints with a different condition

references.title: R2MED: A Benchmark for Reasoning-Driven Medical Retrieval

query.text: [Chief Complaint]: A 28-year-old pregnant woman presents for evaluation. [Current Medical History]: Pulse 112/min; respiratory rate 32/min; blood pressure 162/112mmHg. Normal development, medium nutrition, mental status confused, fearful expression, brought to the ward by stretcher, uncooperative during examination. No jaundice, rash, or purpura on the skin and mucous membranes, no superficial lymphadenopathy, no cranial deformity, bilateral pupils equal and round, approximately 0.3cm, with bris

positive_document.text: [Case Presentation] Chief Complaint Female, 29 years old, office worker 36+1 weeks amenorrhea, headache with lower abdominal pain for 1 day Present Illness Pregnant woman, G1P0. The patient has a history of regular menstruation. Menarche at age 12, menstrual cycle 25-30 days, menstrual period 5-7 days, moderate menstrual flow, occasional dysmenorrhea. LMP: 2016-07-11, EDC: 2017-4-18. Early pregnancy symptoms included mild nausea and vomiting. Fetal movement was felt from the 4th month of pregnancy and continued thereafter. The patient has had regular prenatal check-ups at this hospital. Mid-pregnancy Down's screening indicated low risk. Subsequent OGTT, Down's screening, and ultrasound showed no significant abnormalities. At 36 weeks of prenatal examination, the patient's blood pressure increased to 162/103 mmHg, accompanied by headache and mild lower abdominal pain, without palpitations, chest tightness, or visual disturbances, and no vaginal bleeding or discharge. query.text: [Chief Complaint]: A 70-year-old male patient. Chief Reason: Intermittent dizziness and left upper limb numbness and weakness for 3 days. [Present Illness]: The patient presented with intermittent dizziness without any obvious cause, no vertigo, no headache or vomiting, accompanied by left upper limb numbness and weakness, difficulty in holding objects with the left hand, and normal movement in the other limbs. Since the onset of the illness, his general condition has been acceptable, with no fe

positive_document.text: [Case Presentation] Chief Complaint Male, 69 years old, farmer Chief complaint: Dizziness, numbness and weakness in the right limb for 10 hours, admitted to the hospital. Current Medical History The patient presented with dizziness 10 hours prior to admission without any obvious cause, without vertigo, accompanied by numbness and weakness in the right limb, without nausea, vomiting, headache, tinnitus, facial asymmetry, or speech difficulties. He did not receive any special treatment at home and requested hospitalization. Head CT examination showed: infarct and softening foci in the left basal ganglia, and a softening focus in the right basal ganglia and corona radiata. He was admitted to the hospital with a diagnosis of "cerebral infarction." Since the onset of the illness, the patient's mental status has been acceptable, diet and sleep have been normal, bowel and bladder functions have been regular, physical strength has decreased, and there has been no significant change in body wei

query.text: [Chief Complaint]: Male, 63 years old, farmer Admitted due to speech impairment and right-sided limb weakness for 3 hours. [Current Medical History]: Three hours before admission, the patient presented with speech impairment without any obvious cause, accompanied by right-sided limb weakness, inability to bear weight or walk, no dizziness, no severe headache, no tinnitus, no nausea, vomiting, no palpitations, sweating, no chest tightness, chest pain, no convulsions, no loss of consciousness, no

positive_document.text: [Case Presentation] Chief Complaint Male, 75 years old, farmer Right limb movement restriction for 1 hour Present History The patient reports that 1 hour before admission, he suddenly developed symptoms of right limb movement restriction without any obvious cause, unable to walk independently, no nausea or vomiting, no headache or dizziness, no fever, no blurred vision. He was given oral medication at home, but the symptoms did not significantly improve. He came to our hospital for further examination and was admitted for inpatient treatment. Cranial CT showed: cerebral infarction. Since the onset of the disease, the patient's mental state, appetite, and sleep have been acceptable, bowel and bladder function is normal, and there has been no significant change in weight recently. Past Medical History The patient has a history of hypertension for 4 years, controlled with oral antihypertensive No. 0, with unknown blood pressure control.
